reviews.review_msg: Loved the lectures and the topics/class discussion. Very interesting course.

Stuart is a very fun guy. His classes have a great vibe and you can tell he loves talking to his students. He sparks meaningful discussions in live classes, however my only gripe is that his prerecorded asynchronous lectures are very dull. They are boring and it often feels like he talks about literally nothing for 1 hour.

Dr. Poyntz (Stuart) has by far been one of the best professors I have had the joy to take a class with. His style is highly engaging; both the lectures and readings are easy to follow and can easily be compared to real-life scenarios. A majority of this class is showcasing learning through discussion posts. I thoroughly enjoyed CMNS 221.

Stuart is a truly excellent professor and academic. He is very eloquent and always prepared, making his lectures very interesting and engaging. He is rooting for his students and wants them to do well - even beyond the classroom, Stuart strives for success for his students who reach out.

By far my favourite course ever taken at SFU. Stuart is sooooo inspiring and intelligent. He seems to talk a lot, however he ends class earlier which is awesome. The material covered in lecture was super relevant and relatable. Final assignment was super cool - not your typical paper (but you can if you want to). He wants students to do well!!!!!!!

He uses outdated services (Canvas) to register for course discussions allowing other students to erase names. This happened to me & my friend more than 3 times and Stuart did nothing about it claiming this is the 'first time in 7 years' this has happened. Instead of addressing the issue he blamed Canvas and continued to let these problems occur.

He uses outdated services (Canvas) to register for course discussions allowing other students to erase names. This happened to me & my friend more than 3 times and Stuart did nothing about it claiming this is the 'first time in 7 years' this has happened. Instead of addressing the issue he blamed Canvas and continued to let these problems continue

Stuart is awesome! The content for this course can be dry at times, but he does a great job at making it interesting by showing lots of movie clips and relating it to real world examples. Wants a lot of class participation but isn't awkward about it like some profs can be. Learned about half the class's names by the end of the semester. Love him!

Poyntz is probably the most prepared lecturer I've ever witnessed. He is passionate about what he teaches and he instantly captivates people. He even went to great lengths to bring in Shad, a popular hip-hop artist to come do a guest lecture. I can't wait to take more courses with him!

Believe the hype — this guy is one of the most engaging and well-prepared lecturers I've ever had. It's obvious he's genuinely passionate about the subject matter, and he manages to make even the driest topics interesting without going off on crazy tangents like other profs often do. If you get the chance, take a class with this guy.
Stuart is a really nice and helpful professor. His lectures were so interesting and he has lots of passion on every single lecture. I really like him!!! I would love to take his other courses!!

I new I;d love stuart within the first ten mintutes of the first class. He is really clear and passionate. I learned a lot and I was only paying attention half the time. He's entertaining to listen to and he will ALWAYS reference at least one movie a lecture. great man.
Stuart is incredible. His passion makes the lectures so entertaining and if you are willing, you can learn so much in his classes. Always has great TAs as well!
Stuart is such an interesting and engaging professor. Make sure you go to all the lectures and you will do well on the midterm and final. One of my favorite profs at SFU so far!!

Stuart is an awesome prof. He is really passionate about what he talks about and he uses great contemporary examples. He really cares about his students doing well and encourages questions. Great prof, highly recommended. Only thing, lots of info at each lecture so make sure you attend class. Also, LOTS of readings
Very engaging lecturer! You will do well on the midterm and final if you pay attention in lecture and tutorial.
Very engaging lecturer, and the subject was very interesting. Assignments are fair and if you pay attention to lecture you will do well.

Stuart is INCREDIBLE. Favourite prof at SFU so far. He does absolutely everything he can to get his students interested in the course, and the class is usually completely full. Graham was also an amazing T.A. I look forward to taking more classes with Stuart (and Graham) in the future.
Best prof I had in SFU. Great Lecturer. hope to take him again.

Really great prof! Very enthusiastic about his work and engaging lectures.  There might only be a problem if the material doesnt interest you, but the information is definitely all relevant, depending on if you care or not. Exams are depend pretty heavily on the readings so you probably want to at least skim over and recognize the key concepts.

Stuart is by far the best prof i've ever had! highly recommended. he's very interesting and super easy to understand, and the course material was great. he shows a lot of youtube videos, which is pretty cool too.

Omg, best prof ever! So cool, nice, SUCH a good dresser. I could listen to him talk forever, and theres something about him that makes him hot. I loved actally going to class just to listen to him, its a great course too, very interesting and gives you room to explore CMNS topics. Boring crap TA's though, but wow stuart was great!
LOVED HIM!!! he is a very good prof and is willing to help. Extremely empathetic to students. This is a great course to take with him, you can tell he has the passion to teach.
GREAT LECTURER. Too bad the course is incredibly boring. He tried his best to make it interesting though. TA marked too hard though. Textbook was also very boring.

All of his lectures are uploaded and everything said can be found in the readings. A not-too-hard course, as long as you listen and follow the readings. No surprises on Midterm or Final. It was an okay course, but not spectacular. Probably because my TA wasn't all that into the tutorial, but was nice in general.
Amazing prof. Amazing public speaker as well. Very interesting course. Amazing help in office hours as well. Take this course if you want a GPA booster.

Alright, if you want a grade booster, this is THE course. Super easy course; materials are somewhat interesting yet useless; the best thing is that the sample MT he gives out is what he actually gives you on the MT, and he clearly tells you what he tests you on the final on his review session. Highly recommended as a grade booster!
STUART POYNTZ IS THE BOMB
Good guy, good professor, helpful, engaging during lectures, passionate and was very open to questions during the final prep session. Recommended!
Awesome prof! clearly loves to teach and his lectures are engaging.
Knowledgeable, articulate, engaging- everything you'd want in a professor.

Stuart is the BEST lecturer I have ever had!!!  He is so fascinating when he talks, I loved going to his lectures.  The course itself is not so difficult either, just make sure to do the readings and attend your tutorials.  Start the final paper early, some of the topics are unrelated to the course content.  He shows movie clips during lecture. :-D

Stuart is a really genuine, great guy.  Really knowledgable and passionate about media literacy.  I don't know what those two were talking about before, but talk to him for 5 minutes and you can see that he knows his stuff.

I took CMNS 110 and 221 with Stuart and he's by far one of my favorite profs at sfu, very clear and engaging, does everything in his power to keep his examples current and up to date. MT and final, no surprises, if you pay attention you will definitely be able to pass. Term papers gives you a lot of freedom for exploration. Good feedback also.
Passionate about topic and well-informed.  One of the most interesting 200 level cmns courses.
didnt like cmns, but liked him. makes sure students understand and tried to keep the subject interesting. no suprises on mt or final. he's a nice guy

He made class fun and interesting most days. The readings were interesting for the most part, but he still was very engaged and wanted students to be engaged which is a lot better than most profs. class was relatively easy if you pay attention to lectures and main themes of the readings (i.e. the notes) the exams are pretty easy and straightforward

honestly one of the nicest profs. cares about students and does his best to make  dry, boring material understood & intersting. very accommodating for additional help.  final and mt had no surprises, right from lecture slides & easy 10 pg essay. dont like cmns, but like this prof

He's awesome! So passionate about the class and entertaining to listen to. Gives you a great set of notes to go along with his lectures and is always ready and willing to help when you have a question. I really enjoyed this course!

You know what? You guys are bitter. He is one of the first profs at SFU who has made me passionate about a subject! He approaches his students on a first name basis and even emailed me with suggestions of references I should check out for my term paper. He is not a garbage prof at all. He is enlightening and passionate about what he teaches and kno
